title: Evaluate inverse trig functions
description: Given a sine, cosine, or tangent value, find the principle angle who has this value. In other words, find the arcsine, arccosine, or arctangent of that value.
topic_path: Get ready for differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions

assessment_items.question_content:
The following are all angle measures (in radians, rounded to the nearest hundredth) whose sine is $0.43$.

**Which is the *principal value* of $\sin^{-1}\left(0.43\right)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background

The *principal value* of an inverse trigonometric function is defined over a specific range. In this case, $\sin^{-1}(x)$ is defined as the angle $-\dfrac{\pi}{2}\leq\theta\leq\dfrac{\pi}{2}$ such that $\sin(\theta)=x$. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1405fba87a5165c707481481e2e2af3a06af1b5)

###The answer

The only measure out of the options that is within the interval $\left[-\dfrac{\pi}{2},\dfrac{\pi}{2}\right]$ is $0.44$.

Therefore, $\sin^{-1}\left(0.43\right)=0.44$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following are all angle measures (in radians, rounded to the nearest hundredth) whose sine is $0.98$.

**Which is the *principal value* of $\arcsin\left(0.98\right)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background

The *principal value* of an inverse trigonometric function is defined over a specific range. In this case, $\arcsin(x)$ is defined as the angle $-\dfrac{\pi}{2}\leq\theta\leq\dfrac{\pi}{2}$ such that $\sin(\theta)=x$. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1405fba87a5165c707481481e2e2af3a06af1b5)

###The answer

The only measure out of the options that is within the interval $\left[-\dfrac{\pi}{2},\dfrac{\pi}{2}\right]$ is $1.37$.

Therefore, $\arcsin\left(0.98\right)=1.37$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following are all angle measures (in radians, rounded to the nearest hundredth) whose cosine is $0.32$.

**Which is the *principal value* of $\cos^{-1}\left(0.32\right)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background

The *principal value* of an inverse trigonometric function is defined over a specific range. In this case, $\cos^{-1}(x)$ is defined as the angle $0\leq\theta\leq\pi$ such that $\cos(\theta)=x$. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc741df62f90376a558f3bb47f9ca206031dd39a)

###The answer

The only measure out of the options that is within the interval $\left[0,\pi\right]$ is $1.25$.

Therefore, $\cos^{-1}\left(0.32\right)=1.25$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following are all angle measures (in degrees, rounded to the nearest tenth) whose sine is $0.36$.

**Which is the *principal value* of $\arcsin\left(0.36\right)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background

The *principal value* of an inverse trigonometric function is defined over a specific range. In this case, $\arcsin(x)$ is defined as the angle $-90^\circ\leq\theta\leq90^\circ$ such that $\sin(\theta)=x$. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e73cdd23fed4c7f0756539cd476be73f56dcd463)

###The answer

The only measure out of the options that is within the interval $\left[-90^\circ,90^\circ\right]$ is $21.1^\circ$.

Therefore, $\arcsin\left(0.36\right)=21.1^\circ$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following are all angle measures, in degrees, whose sine is $1$.

**Which is the *principal value* of $\sin^{-1}\left(1\right)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background

The *principal value* of an inverse trigonometric function is defined over a specific range. In this case, $\sin^{-1}(x)$ is defined as the angle $-90^\circ\leq\theta\leq90^\circ$ such that $\sin(\theta)=x$.

###The answer

The only measure out of the options that is within the interval $\left[-90^\circ,90^\circ\right]$ is $90^\circ$.

Therefore, $\sin^{-1}\left(1\right)=90^\circ$.
